source: Telegram Bot API sendMessage response `result` object — a REAL message object (same schema as update.message) captured live from api.telegram.org against the agent's own bot, in a throwaway forum topic (thread 30112) deleted after capture
command: python3 capture-telegram-fixtures.py (urllib POST https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage, then json.dumps of the raw result)
redactions: group chat id and bot user id digit-masked same-length (structural bytes untouched)
note: The server-authored ratification enumeration shape (spec autonomous-scope-accretion-completion.md R37). Sender is the bot (is_bot:true) because the Bot API can only author bot messages; the parser under test reads text/message_id/reply_to_message only — sender verification is a separate, unit-tested layer.
